 {"id":"franken_networkx-dl39","title":"parity: subgraph should reflect parent node removals across graph classes","description":"Graph-family subgraph() currently behaves like a stale snapshot when nodes are removed from the parent graph. After H = G.subgraph([...]), removing node 'a' from G immediately removes that node and its edges from NetworkX subgraph views, but FrankenNetworkX still reports the deleted node and its incident edges. Match the upstream live parent-removal contract across Graph, DiGraph, MultiGraph, and MultiDiGraph.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:57:42.866227258Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T01:58:35.489258849Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T01:58:35.487320369Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-dl59","title":"Pajek round-trip compatibility: support NetworkX writer expectations or document the gap","description":"tests/python/test_round_trip_identity.py now explicitly skips Pajek round-trip coverage because fnx.write_pajek delegates to networkx.write_pajek, which expects G.nodes.get(...) on the Python surface. FrankenNetworkX NodeView currently lacks that mapping-style method, so Pajek round-trips are not currently testable through the NetworkX writer path. -{"id":"franken_networkx-dm4o","title":"parity: Graph.to_directed() should preserve NetworkX directed edge order","description":"Graph.to_directed() currently materializes directed edges in a different observable order than upstream NetworkX. On undirected insertion sequence a-b, b-c, d-a, FrankenNetworkX yields directed edges [('c','b'), ('a','d'), ('a','b'), ('b','a'), ('d','a'), ('b','c')] while NetworkX yields [('a','b'), ('a','d'), ('b','a'), ('b','c'), ('c','b'), ('d','a')].  {"id":"franken_networkx-dmtb","title":"Route graph_edit_distance through local edit-path boundary","description":"Remove the direct NetworkX graph_edit_distance fallback by deriving graph_edit_distance from local optimal_edit_paths behavior, leaving the exact edit-path engine as the only remaining delegated graph edit surface.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T06:00:16.446261048Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T06:01:56.482837822Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T06:01:56.480880600Z","close_reason":"Removed direct NetworkX graph_edit_distance fallback via local optimal_edit_paths boundary","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-do1","title":"Traversal Extras: bfs_beam_edges, bfs/dfs_labeled_edges, generic_bfs_edges","description":"## Scope
Implement 4 advanced traversal functions:
- bfs_beam_edges(G, source, value, width=None) — BFS with beam search pruning. At each level, only expand the top-width nodes as ranked by value(node). Returns edges of the beam-search tree. Useful for exploring large graphs with a heuristic.
- bfs_labeled_edges(G, source, sort_neighbors=None) — yield (u, v, label) where label is one of 'tree' (tree edge in BFS tree), 'level' (edge to node at same level), 'forward' (edge to already-visited node at deeper level), 'reverse' (back edge). Uses BFS with level tracking.
- dfs_labeled_edges(G, source=None, depth_limit=None) — yield (u, v, label) where label is 'tree' (DFS tree edge), 'forward' (edge to descendant not via tree), 'back' (edge to ancestor), 'cross' (edge to node in different subtree), 'nontree' (any non-tree edge). Standard DFS classification.
- generic_bfs_edges(G, source, neighbors=None, depth_limit=None, sort_neighbors=None) — BFS with customizable neighbor function. neighbors(node) returns iterable of adjacent nodes, allowing filtered/weighted BFS.

## Implementation Strategy
- All are generators (yield edges as discovered)
- bfs_labeled: track discovery level per node, classify edges by level comparison
- dfs_labeled: track discovery/finish times, classify by back/forward/cross rules
- generic_bfs: same as bfs_edges but calls neighbors(node) instead of G.neighbors(node)
- bfs_beam: at each level, sort candidates by value(), keep top width

## Test Requirements
- Tree edges form a valid spanning tree (connected, n-1 edges)
- Back edges in DFS create cycles
- Forward edges go to deeper nodes, cross edges go to different subtrees
- bfs_beam with width=None equals regular BFS
- generic_bfs with default neighbors equals regular BFS
- NX cross-validation: same edge classifications on small directed graphs","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-22T17:34:37.942681144Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T22:38:06.367225188Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T22:38:06.366727547Z","close_reason":"Implemented 4 traversal functions: bfs_beam_edges, bfs_labeled_edges, dfs_labeled_edges, generic_bfs_edges.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["algorithms","traversal"]}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-do6g","title":"[RW] GraphML defaults apply even if keys appear after graph","description":"GraphML streaming parser applies key defaults only when node/edge is parsed. If <key> defaults appear after <graph>, defaults are never applied. Fix by applying defaults after parse to all nodes/edges missing those keys and add regression test.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T12:05:50.836706174Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T12:13:17.392794222Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T12:13:17.392533559Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -14521,6 +14521,26 @@ def subgraph(self, nodes):
     return subgraph
 
 
+def _graph_to_directed_copy(self):
+    result = self.to_directed_class()()
+    result.graph.update(deepcopy(self.graph))
+    result.add_nodes_from((node, deepcopy(attrs)) for node, attrs in self.nodes(data=True))
+    for source in self:
+        for target, attrs in self[source].items():
+            result.add_edge(source, target, **deepcopy(attrs))
+    return result
+
+
+def _graph_to_directed_with_view(to_directed_impl):
+    @wraps(to_directed_impl)
+    def to_directed(self, as_view=False):
+        if as_view is True:
+            return _generic_directed_graph_view(self)
+        return _graph_to_directed_copy(self)
+
+    return to_directed
+
+
 def _to_directed_with_view(to_directed_impl):
     @wraps(to_directed_impl)
     def to_directed(self, as_view=False):
@@ -14593,7 +14613,7 @@ def to_undirected(self, reciprocal=False, as_view=False):

 MultiGraph.subgraph = _subgraph_with_view(_MULTIGRAPH_SUBGRAPH)
 MultiDiGraph.subgraph = _subgraph_with_view(_MULTIDIGRAPH_SUBGRAPH)
-Graph.to_directed = _to_directed_with_view(_GRAPH_TO_DIRECTED)
+Graph.to_directed = _graph_to_directed_with_view(_GRAPH_TO_DIRECTED)
 DiGraph.to_directed = _to_directed_with_view(_DIGRAPH_TO_DIRECTED)
 MultiGraph.to_directed = _to_directed_with_view(_MULTIGRAPH_TO_DIRECTED)
 MultiDiGraph.to_directed = _to_directed_with_view(_MULTIDIGRAPH_TO_DIRECTED)

@@ -1516,6 +1516,24 @@ def test_to_directed_matches_networkx_without_fallback(monkeypatch, fnx_cls, nx_
     assert _graph_snapshot(result) == expected_snapshot
 
 
+def test_graph_to_directed_preserves_networkx_directed_edge_order(monkeypatch):
+    graph = fnx.Graph()
+    expected = nx.Graph()
+    for current in (graph, expected):
+        current.add_edge("a", "b")
+        current.add_edge("b", "c")
+        current.add_edge("d", "a")
+
+    expected_result = expected.to_directed()
+
+    _block_networkx_utilities(monkeypatch, "to_directed")
+
+    result = graph.to_directed()
+
+    assert list(result.edges()) == list(expected_result.edges())
+    assert list(result.edges(data=True)) == list(expected_result.edges(data=True))
+
+
 @pytest.mark.parametrize(
     ("fnx_cls", "nx_cls"),
